_schema_note: 3-section schema: weekly_strategy (current + 2 weeks), tracking (running experiments + metric variance), backlog (full queue with execution steps).

weekly_strategy.thesis: Ads API just went live (Apr 26). One keyword consumed 46% of spend with $0 sales (`stainless steel cat water fountain`). Cutting bleeder bid + adding 5 missing keywords + standing up filter PPC should compound to <35% ACoS within 7 days.

experiments.title: Kill SP_Test_Expansion + fold KW into Manual

weekly_strategy.thesis: Dog SV is 2-3x cat SV. We can't bid on dog terms profitably without listing support. May 4 also lands as the EXP-046/047 7-day decision date.

experiments.title: Wave 3 listing copy — small dog/multi-pet bridge

weekly_strategy.thesis: By May 11 we have 14 days of filter PPC data. If filter PPC works, scale it. EXP-050 launches dog PPC validation against Wave 3 listing.

tracking.title: Bid surgery on 6 active keywords
tracking.expected: Cut $10/day from bleeder while protecting winner. Portfolio ACoS 44% → <35% in 7 days.
tracking.actual: Day 0. Bid changes pending Victor's apply in Campaign Manager. Tomorrow 7am cron pulls Apr 27 data — first measurable signal.
tracking.learning: First time per-keyword ACoS drives bid decisions instead of campaign-aggregate. Validates if a single-day cadence is responsive enough or if 7d window is right.

tracking.title: Add 5 missing exact-match cat keywords
tracking.expected: Add ~$3-5/day spend at 30-40% ACoS. 1+ conversion across 5 new keywords in 7d.
tracking.learning: If H10 search-volume estimates correlate with real impressions captured.

tracking.title: Filter cross-sell campaign launch (B0FWXJ1GKT)
tracking.learning: Whether branded-search filter buyers exist in volume sufficient to justify scaling.

tracking.title: Request-a-Review systematic campaign
tracking.expected: 2-3 new reviews in 7 days. Recover Amazon's Choice badge.
tracking.actual: Stars stable at 4.5 but no new reviews this week. Badge LOST since Apr 20. 4 fountain + 2 filter Apr 25 buyers are next-up requests.
tracking.learning: Without a workflow to send reviews on schedule, the ask drifts. Need to systematize the Apr 25 batch this week.

tracking.title: Bundle copy decision (rewrite or deactivate)
tracking.expected: 1+ bundle sale per week sustained, OR trigger copy rewrite.
tracking.actual: Apr 11-17 had 1 sale; Apr 18-26 had 0. The single sale was an outlier — no follow-on demand at current copy.
tracking.learning: Title that buries the value prop produces single accidental conversions but no sustained demand. Trigger rewrite Apr 29.

tracking.title: Aged inventory removal decision
tracking.expected: Decision: absorb $128.84 surcharge OR remove N units before May 1.
tracking.actual: Velocity improved 0.37/day → 0.71/day = days-of-cover halved 1008 → 518. But still 1.4 years of cover. Surcharge looms.
tracking.learning: Velocity is improving with PPC discipline. Removal becomes harder to justify if trend continues — decide before May 1.

backlog.title: Bid surgery on 6 active keywords
backlog.assumption: One keyword (`stainless steel cat water fountain`) is consuming 46% of Apr 26 PPC spend with $0 sales. Cutting its bid by 31% and raising winner bids by 11-21% should compound to ~30% portfolio ACoS within 7 days.
backlog.rationale: Apr 26 Ads API gave us per-keyword ACoS for the first time. Bleeder has 1358 impressions / 5 clicks / 0 conversions = poor relevance. Cut bid first; if still 0 conversions in 7d at $1.10, pause entirely.
backlog.expected_change: Daily spend $22 → $15-18. Portfolio ACoS 44% → <35%. Winner gains 30-50% more impression share.
execution_steps: Open Seller Central > Campaigns > Sponsored Products > Manual Keywords Campaign > Phrase Match ad group
Find `stainless steel cat water fountain` — change bid $1.60 → $1.10
Find `cat fountain stainless steel` — change bid $1.80 → $2.00
Find `cat water fountain stainless steel` — change bid $1.49 → $1.80
Save. Daily 7am cron will pull new ACoS data automatically.

backlog.title: Add 5 missing exact-match cat keywords
backlog.assumption: These 5 keywords (~14K combined SV) have low title density and weak competitor ranks. The strategy doc assumed they were running but Ads API confirmed they are NOT. Adding at conservative bids should yield 1-2 incremental conversions/wk.
backlog.rationale: We rank #160+ organically, so PPC is the only access. Conservative bids prevent runaway spend. If any keyword gets 0 impressions in 3 days, raise +20%.
backlog.expected_change: +$3-5/day spend at 30-40% ACoS. 1-2 incremental units/wk.
execution_steps: Open Manual Keywords Campaign > Phrase Match ad group > Add keywords
Add as exact match with these bids:
• wireless cat water fountain — $0.90
• cordless cat water fountain — $1.10
• cat water fountain wireless — $1.00
• cat water fountain cordless — $1.00
• metal cat water fountain — $1.20
Save. Verify all 5 show 'Active' status.
Confirm campaign-level negatives (pumpless, filterless, ceramic, no filter) are scoped here.

backlog.title: Filter cross-sell campaign launch (B0FWXJ1GKT)
backlog.assumption: Filter ASIN has 0 PPC, 2 organic units/7d, 43% gross margin (vs fountain 33%). Branded filter searches like `veken cat water fountain filters` (SV 3,256) have intent. $5/day at $0.80 exact bid = 1-2 sales/wk.
backlog.rationale: Filter is the razor blade in the razor-blade model. Current ratio 0.27 — well below industry standard 0.6+. PPC on filters is the cheapest scale lever and the buyer cohort already converted on the fountain.
backlog.expected_change: $5/day spend → 1-2 filter sales/wk at 30-50% ACoS. Razor-blade ratio 0.27 → 0.35+ within 30 days.
execution_steps: Seller Central > Campaigns > Create campaign > Sponsored Products > Manual targeting
Campaign name: Filters_Manual_2026-04-28
Daily budget: $5
ASIN: B0FWXJ1GKT
Targeting: Manual > Keyword targeting > Exact match
Add keyword: `veken cat water fountain filters` at $0.80 bid
Add negatives at campaign level: `pawira filter`, `petlibro filter`, `paws & pals filter`
Save and launch.

backlog.title: Request-a-Review for Apr 25 buyers
backlog.assumption: Apr 25 had 6 orders (4 fountain + 2 filter). Sending Request-a-Review on the 5-day post-delivery mark catches buyers when satisfaction signal is fresh. 2-3 new reviews moves us from 21 → 23-24, closing the gap to badge recovery.
backlog.rationale: Amazon's Choice badge LOST since Apr 20. Without it, organic CTR drops 15-25%. Reviews are the controllable input.
backlog.expected_change: +2-3 reviews in 7 days. Move toward 25+ review threshold for badge recovery.
execution_steps: Open Seller Central > Orders > Manage Orders
Filter to Apr 25 ship date
For each of 6 orders, click order → Contact buyer → 'Request a Review' (one-click button)
Schedule any buyer past their 5-day delivery mark today; rest at delivery+5
Log in cash_balance.json or new reviews_log.json with date sent.

backlog.title: Bundle copy rewrite (deactivate decision)
backlog.assumption: Apr 11-17 had 1 sale (outlier); Apr 18-26 had 0. Two consecutive zero-sale weeks confirms title buries the value prop. Rewrite leading with 'Starter Kit + Save' framing should drive 1+ sale within 14d.
backlog.rationale: Bundle is the highest-margin item in the catalog and unlocks the filter subscription flywheel. Zero-sale weeks indicate copy problem, not demand problem (filter sells separately).
backlog.expected_change: 1+ bundle sale within 14 days OR formal deactivation (EXP-036).
execution_steps: Open existing experiment_log.json EXP-040 execution block for full new title + bullets text
Seller Central > Inventory > B0GMDM6CG2 > Edit
Replace title with new copy. Replace bullets 1-5.
Submit. Approval ~24h.
If 0 sales by May 13, formally deactivate via EXP-036.

backlog.title: Aged inventory removal decision (May 1 surcharge)
backlog.assumption: Velocity improved 0.37 → 0.71/day this week. If trend holds, 365-day cover threshold is reachable in ~6 weeks. Absorbing the $128.84 surcharge is cheaper than removal fees IF velocity holds.
backlog.rationale: Removal cost ~$0.50/unit + outbound shipping. 150 units removed = ~$100. Surcharge $128.84 once. Marginal savings unless velocity stalls.
backlog.expected_change: Decision documented Apr 30: absorb surcharge (default) OR remove ~150 units.
execution_steps: Open Seller Central > Inventory > Aged Inventory Surcharge report
Confirm exact surcharge amount and effective date
Pull last 14d daily velocity from PL_Data files
If velocity > 0.6/day: absorb surcharge
If velocity < 0.5/day: submit FBA removal order for ~150 units before midnight Apr 30

backlog.title: Wave 3 listing copy — small dog/multi-pet bridge
backlog.assumption: Dog buyers bounce on cat-only listing copy. Adding multi-pet imagery + bullet 4 mention will let dog PPC (EXP-050) convert without harming cat CVR.
backlog.rationale: Cerebro shows dog SV is 2-3x cat SV. We cannot bid on dog terms profitably until the listing answers 'will this work for my dog?' in the first scroll.
backlog.expected_change: Unblocks EXP-050. Holds cat CVR within ±0.5pt of pre-publish baseline.
execution_steps: Seller Central > Inventory > B0DJHQVJYF > Edit
Bullet 4: append '...also great for small dogs and multi-pet households — 67oz capacity serves multiple drinkers'
A+ Content: upload 1 image of small dog drinking from fountain (replace decorative cat image)
Submit for review. Approval 24-48h.
After live: do NOT touch cat-side bullets/title. Stability protects re-earning Amazon's Choice.

backlog.title: Dog PPC validation launch
backlog.assumption: Once Wave 3 listing publishes, bidding on 2 phrase-match dog keywords at $0.60 with $5/day cap should yield 1-2 dog-attributed sales in 14d at <60% ACoS. Above 60%, kill.
backlog.rationale: Dog SV 2-3x cat. If even 30% of clicks convert, we unlock a market larger than current cat focus.
backlog.expected_change: 1+ dog-attributed sale in 14 days. ACoS data validates whether to scale or kill dog targeting.
execution_steps: BLOCKED: confirm EXP-049 listing copy is published live before launching
Seller Central > Campaigns > Create campaign > Sponsored Products > Manual targeting
Campaign name: SP_Dog_Validation_2026-05-11
Daily budget: $5 (HARD CAP)
Add keywords (phrase match): `dog water fountain stainless steel` $0.60, `stainless steel dog water fountain` $0.60
Add negatives: `cat`, `kitten`, `kitty`
Save and launch.
Monitor daily. If 14d ACoS > 60%, pause.

backlog.title: Image overlay — feature callouts on hero image
backlog.assumption: Hero image is generic. Adding 3 callouts (`67oz`, `Wireless`, `Stainless Steel`) overlay should lift CTR 10-15%.
backlog.rationale: Wave 4 element. Standard practice in pet category. Competitors all use overlays.
backlog.expected_change: CTR +10-15% within 14 days of new image going live.
execution_steps: Design overlay variant in Canva (3 callouts, brand colors)
Export PNG 2000x2000
Seller Central > Inventory > B0DJHQVJYF > Manage Images > replace position 1
Submit. ~6h propagation.

backlog.title: Bullet rewrite — convert visitors into buyers
backlog.assumption: Current bullets describe features. Rewrite with benefit-led structure (problem → solution → proof) lifts CVR from 3.4% → 4.5%+.
backlog.rationale: Wave 3 priority experiment. Includes EXP-031 + EXP-033 sub-themes (price-anchor + filter-frequency).
backlog.expected_change: CVR 3.4% → 4.5%+ within 14d of new bullets going live.
execution_steps: See experiment_log.json EXP-022 execution block for full copy-paste bullets
Edit listing in Seller Central
Submit. 24h approval.

backlog.title: Launch Filters_Manual campaign — first-ever PPC on B0FWXJ1GKT
backlog.assumption: Filter cross-sell at 45% margin can sustain higher ACoS than fountain (9% margin). Top-SV filter terms (cat water fountain filter SV 28K, cat fountain filter SV 18K) have 0-1 competitors in top 30 organic — uncontested PPC space.
backlog.rationale: Filter B0FWXJ1GKT has had $0 in PPC spend ever. Cerebro Apr 28 reveals 4 high-SV filter KWs with low competitor density. Razor-blade margin economics + uncontested terms = highest expected ROI per dollar in the entire portfolio.
backlog.expected_change: Filter monthly unit sales ~50% lift over current organic-only baseline. Hard ACoS gate at 30% (vs 33.5% breakeven on fountain — filters have headroom).
execution_steps: Seller Central > Create Campaign > Sponsored Products > Manual targeting > Continue
Campaign name: SP_Filters_Manual_2026-04-29
Daily budget: $5.00
Bidding strategy: Dynamic bids — down only
Placement: Top of search 0%, Product pages 0%
Ad group: Filters_Exact, Default bid: $0.80
Add product: B0FWXJ1GKT
Add keywords (EXACT match):
cat water fountain filter — $0.80
cat fountain filter — $0.80
veken water fountain filters — $0.80
petlibro water fountain filter — $0.65
Launch campaign. Monitor daily for first 3 days; verify ACoS < 30% by 2026-05-06.

success_criteria.how_to_measure: Ads API filter campaign report — pull on 2026-05-06

backlog.title: Add 3 momentum-validated cat KWs to Manual Keywords Campaign
backlog.assumption: 6-month rank history (KT export Apr 29) shows we're GAINING organic rank on `cat water fountain stainless steel wireless` (#147 → #86, +61), `rechargeable cat water fountain` (#255 → #166, +89), and `kitty spout` is brand-search arbitrage. Bidding here amplifies a winning algorithmic signal.
backlog.rationale: Per the lessons file (Apr 29): bid HIGHER on KWs we're already gaining organic rank on (algo reinforcement); avoid pouring money into broad terms where we're losing ground. These 3 KWs satisfy that rule. Replaces the original `stainless steel cat water fountain` plan, which the rank history invalidates.
backlog.expected_change: 3 new exact-match KWs convert at 2-3x current portfolio CVR because they're niche-specific. Adds ~$3-5/day spend, expected to recover via 1-2 incremental orders/week.
execution_steps: Seller Central > Sponsored Products > Manual Keywords Campaign > add keywords
Add (EXACT match):
cat water fountain stainless steel wireless — bid $1.30
rechargeable cat water fountain — bid $1.00
kitty spout cat water fountain — bid $1.20
Save. First ACoS read on 2026-05-02; verdict on 2026-05-06.

success_criteria.how_to_measure: Ads API SP Targeting report — count attributed orders for the 3 new KWs

backlog.title: Bulk-negate 26 wasted-spend terms flagged by Cerebro
backlog.assumption: Cerebro Apr 28 flagged 26 KWs containing 'ceramic', 'no filter', 'pumpless', etc — wrong product attribute matches. Adding as negatives saves PPC dollars on clicks that will not convert.
backlog.rationale: We are paying for impressions/clicks on KWs that semantically can't sell our product (e.g. 'ceramic cat water fountain' — we're stainless). Per lessons (Apr 18 / EXP-024): every negative we should have added but didn't is a daily leak.
backlog.expected_change: Daily wasted spend down ~$2-4. ACoS improvement of 3-5pp portfolio-wide.
execution_steps: Open dashboard > PPC Strategy > H10 Opportunities panel > Negatives filter
Click 'Add as negatives' button — routes to experiments draft flow with all 26 terms
OR direct: Seller Central > Manual Keywords Campaign > Negative Keywords > paste in the 26 terms (full list in cerebro_opportunities_2026-04-28.json under tag=negative)
Save. Effect compounds daily — verify wasted spend reduction on 2026-05-06.

success_criteria.how_to_measure: Dashboard Strategy > Wasted Spend (7d) block — should drop

backlog.title: Filter listing audit + refresh (B0FWXJ1GKT) — images, A+, copy
backlog.assumption: Filter listing currently has weak images and minimal A+ content. With Filters_Manual campaign launching Apr 29, every dollar of new traffic will convert poorly until the listing matches buyer intent (compatibility, freshness, multi-pack value).
backlog.rationale: Filter is 45% margin and razor-blade economics depend on it converting. Driving PPC to a weak listing is throwing money into a sieve. Listing improvements compound with PPC traffic — get them shipping in parallel.
backlog.expected_change: Filter session-to-order CVR from current baseline (TBD — needs SP-API session pull) to +30%. A+ + better images = better organic indexing too.
execution_steps: Step 1 (Claude): Live scrape of B0FWXJ1GKT via Chrome — capture current state of: hero image, image carousel (count + content), bullets, A+ modules, comparison table
Step 2 (Claude): Audit against top-converting filter listings (Veken filter, FEELNEEDY filter) — identify gaps in visual hierarchy, social proof, compatibility messaging
Step 3 (Victor): Decide on 3-5 image refreshes (ranked by impact)
Step 4 (Victor): Update via Seller Central > Manage Inventory > Edit listing
Step 5: Verify rank impact in next Sunday's KT pull

success_criteria.how_to_measure: SP-API Business Reports — sessions/units conversion for B0FWXJ1GKT

backlog.title: Bundle listing audit + refresh — images, A+, copy
backlog.assumption: Bundle listing has weak images / A+ / copy. Bundle is at 43% margin and was identified as 'unlaunched' in KW_Data Apr 26. PPC won't be effective without first ensuring the listing converts.
backlog.rationale: Same logic as EXP-054 — listing fundamentals must match marketing intent before paid traffic can compound. Bundle has the highest margin among our SKUs; getting its conversion right multiplies every other PPC effort.
backlog.expected_change: Bundle attach rate (% of fountain orders that include bundle) from current baseline to +25%. Bundle SKU sessions/orders ratio improves.
execution_steps: Step 1 (Claude): Live scrape of bundle ASIN via Chrome — confirm ASIN first, then capture full listing state
Step 2 (Claude): Compare against top-rated bundle competitors in cat fountain category (any competitor selling fountain+filter combo)
Step 3 (Victor): Confirm bundle ASIN and provide listing access
Step 4 (Victor): Update images / A+ / bullets based on audit
Step 5: First impact reading on 2026-05-15

success_criteria.how_to_measure: SP-API Orders — bundle SKU orders / fountain SKU orders

backlog.title: PAT campaign on PETLIBRO B0FDKQGRCK — comparison-shopper interception
backlog.assumption: PETLIBRO sits BSR #5 in Cat Fountains at $66.36 with 1,813 reviews. Buyers comparison-shopping on their listing are exactly the premium-tier segment we want. PAT bids on their detail page can intercept at 30-50% of broad-keyword CPC.
backlog.rationale: Today's Insights tab shows PETLIBRO with Threat Score 50 — third-highest. Premium positioning means their browsers can afford ours. Their 4.3★ vs our 4.5★ is a real wedge.
backlog.expected_change: 5-15 sponsored impressions/day on B0FDKQGRCK detail page, 1-2 clicks/day at <$0.50 CPC, first attributed click within 7 days. Monitor for ACoS — gate at 50% (filters' ACoS gate is 30%; PAT against premium has more headroom because basket size and CVR are higher).
execution_steps: Seller Central > Create Campaign > Sponsored Products > Manual targeting > Continue
Campaign name: SP_PAT_PETLIBRO_2026-04-30
Daily budget: $3.00
Bidding strategy: Dynamic bids — down only
Placement: Top of search 0%, Product pages +20%
Ad group: PAT_PETLIBRO_Dockstream
Default bid: $0.45
Add product: B0DJHQVJYF
Product targeting > Individual products > Enter list:
B0FDKQGRCK    bid $0.45    (PETLIBRO Dockstream 2)
Negative product targeting:
B0DJHQVJYF    (exclude our own)
Launch. Daily check-in for first 3 days. Decision on 2026-05-07.

success_criteria.how_to_measure: Ads API SP Targeting report — filter by campaign SP_PAT_PETLIBRO_2026-04-30

backlog.title: Fountain bullets — quality-positioning rewrite vs APAUK / PawPoll
backlog.assumption: APAUK ($21.99, 4.5★ × 2,502 rev) and PawPoll ($39.99, 4.1★ × 3,668 rev) attack on price. We can't win on price. The lever we DO have: we are 4.5★ — matching APAUK and ABOVE PawPoll. Lead bullets with quality + materials, not feature-list parity.
backlog.rationale: Replaces stale EXP-029 which still assumed CVR investigation was the bottleneck. Apr 28 listing audit + competitor data make the actual answer clear: premium positioning is the only path. Bullets must STOP sounding like every other listing and START sounding like a premium product.
backlog.expected_change: CVR lift 15-25% on premium-intent terms (`stainless steel`, `quiet`, `cordless`). No measurable change on price-shopping terms (`cheap`, `bundle`) — that's intentional, those buyers go to APAUK.
execution_steps: Open Strategy tab > Listing Touchpoint Map for fountain B0DJHQVJYF
Identify which bullets currently mirror competitor language (genericness check)
Rewrite using framework: <PREMIUM-CLAIM> — <CONCRETE-DIFF-VS-COMPETITOR> — <BUYER-OUTCOME>

Example new Bullet 1: 'WHISPER-QUIET 20dB MOTOR — Most fountains hum at 30-40dB. Paw Pure runs at 20dB, the loudness of a quiet library. Won't startle skittish cats, won't wake light sleepers. The premium pump that justifies the price.'

Example new Bullet 2: 'FOOD-GRADE STAINLESS STEEL TRAY — Other brands hide plastic behind a steel rim. The surface your cat's tongue touches is 304 stainless steel — the same grade used in commercial kitchens. Zero plastic taste, BPA-free.'
Submit via Seller Central > Manage Inventory > B0DJHQVJYF > Edit
Wait 24h for live, then verify changes
Monitor SP-API Business Reports for CVR delta over 14 days vs prior 14-day baseline

success_criteria.how_to_measure: SP-API Business Reports + Ads API attributed CVR for KWs containing 'stainless', 'quiet', 'cordless'

backlog.title: Daily Request-a-Review automation via SP-API Solicitations
backlog.assumption: Reviews are THE bottleneck (we have 21 vs APAUK 2,502). At our current ~0.65 reviews/week pace, we'd need 38 years to match APAUK. Solicitations API can request a review on every delivered order, ~2.5x our current organic ask rate.
backlog.rationale: Generalises stale EXP-035 with concrete tactics. The Solicitations API is ready-but-gated per data_freshness notes. Closing this gap unlocks: (1) Amazon's Choice re-eligibility, (2) organic CVR floor, (3) ladder pricing milestones in pricingLadder.
backlog.expected_change: Review velocity from 0.65/wk → 2/wk within 30 days. Hit 50 reviews by mid-June (currently 21 + 30 days × 2/wk = 30). Subsequent ladder unlocks $42.99 price point at 50 reviews per strategy_pricing.json.
execution_steps: Verify SP-API Solicitations endpoint is callable (check Auth scope: solicitations.requestReviewSolicitation:create)
If gated: open Seller Central case to request access — usually 24-48h

Build amazon_api/pullers/request_reviews.py: pulls delivered orders from last 4-30 days, filters out already-asked (track in solicitation_log.json), calls POST /solicitations/v1/orders/{orderId}/solicitations/productReviewAndSellerFeedback
Add to daily_pipeline.py to run every morning
Track outcomes in dashboard: new column 'requested' on orders table
Insert thank-you cards in next inventory shipment with QR code → review page (parallel manual lever)

backlog.title: Reports API quota retry + backfill — no more Sessions/CVR gaps
backlog.assumption: Sessions and CVR have been null since Apr 27 (Reports API quota exceeded). The dashboard now uses last-known-good (Apr 23: 157 sessions, 3.18% CVR) but this hides real movement. A retry-with-exponential-backoff inside pull_business_reports.py would close the gap.
backlog.rationale: Daily KPI history shows we're flying blind on the conversion side of the funnel. Without sessions data, we cannot confirm whether the 89.6% ACoS is being caused by traffic drying up OR PPC inefficiency.
backlog.expected_change: Sessions/CVR populated daily within 7 days. 'as of Apr 23 (Reports API quota)' annotation disappears from PPC Strategy tab. EXP-057 success_criteria becomes measurable.
execution_steps: Read amazon_api/pullers/pull_business_reports.py — find quota error handling
Wrap report request in retry-with-exponential-backoff: 1s, 5s, 30s, 5min, 30min — give up after 4 hours total
On quota_exceeded: log to amazon_api/data/logs/reports_quota.log with timestamp
Add cron retry: if pipeline ran but business_reports came back null, queue a retry 4h later
Backfill: scan ads_*.json window dates and try to pull historical Reports data for any missing days (if API allows)
Verify: run scripts/build_daily_kpis_history.py after first successful pull — gaps should fill in

success_criteria.how_to_measure: Count of dates in daily_kpis_history.series where sessions != null in last 7 days

backlog.title: Wave 3 dog bullet copy — ship the concrete bridge for Phase 3
backlog.assumption: EXP-049 exists but is undated and lacks the actual copy. Phase 3 dog PPC (EXP-050) is gated on this. Today's competitor data (oneisall $175K/mo at 10L) confirms we cannot compete on capacity but CAN on wireless + multi-pet positioning.
backlog.rationale: Concrete version of EXP-049 with real copy. Without this, dog SVs (57K/57K/33K) stay un-bid and Phase 3 cannot start.
backlog.expected_change: Listing indexes for dog/multi-pet KWs within 7 days. Unlocks EXP-050 dog PPC validation.
execution_steps: Update Bullet 4 of fountain B0DJHQVJYF: replace existing text with

'WORKS FOR CATS AND SMALL DOGS — 3L/108oz capacity is enough for cats AND small dogs to share. Three flow modes (Gentle Stream, Bubble, Continuous) keep every pet drinking — even hesitant ones. Multi-pet households, one fountain.'
Update A+ Module 3: add a small dog drinking image alongside cat
Update backend search terms: add 'small dog water fountain wireless', 'cordless dog water fountain', 'multi pet water fountain'
Submit via Seller Central > B0DJHQVJYF > Edit
After 7 days: pull KW_Data, verify dog KWs are now indexed (rank ≤ 306)
If indexed: launch EXP-050 (dog PPC validation) on 2026-05-12

success_criteria.how_to_measure: Count of dog KWs (those matching DOG_REGEX) where rank_history shows organic rank ≤ 306

backlog.title: Kill SP_Test_Expansion + fold `cat drinking fountain` into Manual Keywords Campaign
backlog.growth_metric: Campaign hygiene + impressions on 'cat drinking fountain'
backlog.assumption: SP_Test_Expansion_2026-04-19 has produced 1 impression in 8 days because its single keyword (`cat drinking fountain` exact) is bid too low to win the auction. Per Apr 30 lesson: single-KW low-bid test campaigns are silent failures. Folding the KW into the active Manual Keywords Campaign at competitive bid will capture impressions immediately.
backlog.rationale: Cerebro Apr 28 + KT rank history confirm `cat drinking fountain` (SV 5,519) gained 104 organic positions in the last 30 days (#280 → #176). The Apr 29 lesson says: bid up where the algo is favoring us. Test campaign has zero discoverability; main campaign already wins impressions on similar terms.
backlog.expected_change: Campaign accounting cleaner. `cat drinking fountain` exact gets 5-15 impressions/day inside Manual Keywords Campaign at $0.80 bid. First attributed click within 7 days.
execution_steps: Seller Central > Sponsored Products > Campaigns > select SP_Test_Expansion_2026-04-19
Campaign settings > Pause campaign (do NOT delete — keep history for reference)
Open Manual Keywords Campaign > add keyword:
cat drinking fountain — match: exact — bid: $0.80
Save. Daily monitor for first 3 days; verify impressions > 0 by 2026-05-03.
Add to lessons file: 'Single-KW test campaigns produce zero data unless bid is competitive.'

success_criteria.how_to_measure: Ads API SP Targeting report — filter keyword 'cat drinking fountain'.

backlog.title: Upgrade filter listing (B0FWXJ1GKT) to A+ Premium
backlog.assumption: Bundle (B0GMDM6CG2) and main fountain (B0DJHQVJYF) are both on A+ Premium. Filter (B0FWXJ1GKT) is the only Paw Pure listing still on basic A+ Content. A+ Premium adds video modules, 1000-pixel hero rows, hover-zoom, and comparison tables — all of which lift CVR ~5-15% per Amazon's own benchmarks for sub-$30 SKUs.
backlog.rationale: We just shipped EXP-054 quick fixes (title + bullet 1 sponge count + OEM bullet + multi-layer wording). Filter Campaign EXP-051 starts driving paid traffic this week. Every dollar of new PPC traffic on the filter converts at a higher rate when the listing has Premium A+ vs basic. Closing the gap brings filter listing parity with bundle and main listing.
backlog.expected_change: Filter session-to-order CVR lifts 5-15% post-Premium upgrade. Hover-zoom + 1000px hero rows give buyers more detail before clicking away. Comparison module helps frame Paw Pure vs RIZZARI on real attributes (e.g. compatibility specifically with Paw Pure fountain).
execution_steps: Confirm Brand Registry 2.0 status on Paw Pure brand (required for A+ Premium)
Seller Central > Stores > Manage A+ Content > Create A+ Premium Content
Use the same module library as the bundle/fountain Premium A+ but tailor to filter narrative:
Module 1: Hero with hover-zoom — 'Multi-Layer Filtration Inside'
Module 2: Video module — 30s show-and-tell of swapping a filter (already have footage, edit and upload)
Module 3: 1000px crossover row — three side-by-side panels: (a) cotton mesh, (b) activated carbon, (c) ion-exchange resin — each with a single-line benefit
Module 4: Comparison table — Paw Pure OEM filter vs generic third-party filters on: dimensions match, pump compatibility, multi-layer construction, food-grade materials
